channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: At least 15 people injured after Florida train hits fire truck
item.description: Three firefighters and 12 Brightline passengers were injured Saturday morning following a collision between a Delray Beach Fire Rescue truck and a train.
item.dc:creator: Sierra Rains, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump appears to side with Musk, tech allies in debate over foreign workers roiling his supporters
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump appears to be siding with Elon Musk and his other backers in the tech industry as a dispute over immigration visas has divided his supporters.
item.dc:creator: Michelle L. Price, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: LIST: Top 10 wackiest Florida Man (and Woman) headlines of 2024
item.description: Another year has come and gone, but the wacky antics of Florida residents will live on forever through the internet.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, ABC 4

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYPD investigates after homeless man catches fire in Manhattan subway station
item.description: New York police are investigating after a 67-year-old man caught fire and suffered burn injuries while inside Manhattan's Penn Station. No arrests have been made.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Big Lots reaches deal to keep hundreds of US stores open
item.description: The discount chain Big Lots, which filed for bankruptcy protection in September, has reached a deal that will keep hundreds of its stores and distribution centers open. Big Lots said Friday it will be sold to Gordon Brothers Retail Partners, a firm that specializes in distressed companies.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Musk calls some MAGA supporters ‘contemptible fools’ as visa row intensifies
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk labeled a section of President-elect Trump supporters as “contemptible fools” as the online debate around visas for highly skilled workers on the right intensifies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: After predicting the financial growth of 2024, here's what 2 economists see in 2025
item.description: Brian Blank is a finance scholar and Fed watcher who researches how companies navigate downturns and make financial decisions, as well as how markets process information.
item.dc:creator: KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Olivia Hussey, star of the 1968 film 'Romeo and Juliet,' dies at 73
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Olivia Hussey, the actor who starred as a teenage Juliet in the 1968 film “Romeo and Juliet,” has died, her family said on social media Saturday. She was 73. Hussey died on Friday, “peacefully at home surrounded by her loved ones,” a statement posted to her Instagram account said.
item.dc:creator: SYLVIA HUI, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Putin apologizes for 'tragic incident' but stops short of saying Azerbaijani plane was shot down
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin on Saturday apologized to his Azerbaijani counterpart for what he called a “tragic incident” following the crash of an Azerbaijani airliner in Kazakhstan that killed 38 people, but stopped short of acknowledging that Moscow was responsible.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Winning ticket for $1.22 billion lottery jackpot sold in California, Mega Millions says
item.description: At least one Mega Millions player has plenty of dough to ring in the New Year after drawing the winning number.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Will New Year's Eve be loud or quiet? What are the top 2025 resolutions? AP-NORC poll has answers
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If you're planning on ringing in the new year quietly at home, you're not alone. A majority of U.S adults intend to celebrate New Year’s Eve at home, according to a new poll by The Associated Press-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli soldiers burn a hospital in Gaza
item.description: Israeli soldiers stormed and burned a hospital in isolated northern Gaza after forcibly removing staff and patients on Friday, Health Ministry officials said. The Israeli military said the hospital was being used by Hamas fighters as a base, although it did not provide evidence.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FAA places restrictions on drone company after Florida boy injured at holiday airshow, underwent heart surgery
item.description: A drone company had some of its operations put on hold amid an investigation into an incident in which a boy was injured during a Florida holiday show.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Explosion at Tyson Foods plant leaves 1 dead, others injured
item.description: An explosion at a Tyson Foods plant in Georgia late Thursday night left one person dead and others injured.
item.dc:creator: Kyler Swaim, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Another unticketed passenger attempts to stow away on Delta flight
item.description: In the thick of holiday travel, another unticketed passenger attempted to stow away on a Delta Air Lines flight.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Bird flu virus in Louisiana patient likely mutated to be more transmissible, CDC says
item.description: The agency, in its latest update released Thursday, said the mutations were not found in samples taken from wild birds, suggesting the virus changed over the course of the patient’s infection.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Final crystal triangles installed on Times Square ball ahead of New Year's Eve
item.description: The last of the crystal triangles that make up this year’s Times Square New Year’s Eve ball have been installed, marking the first time in 10 years that all 2,688 were replaced at once.
item.dc:creator: By Melissa Goldin, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Onion-wielding man disrupts Christmas church services, pours whiskey into holy water: deputies
item.description: A Maryland man was arrested and charged with several offences stemming from disruptions at two Catholic churches on Christmas Eve, including one disruption which he is accused of pouring liquor into a container of holy water.
item.dc:creator: Alaina Papazian, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man indicted in burning death of woman inside New York City subway train: prosecutors
item.description: The indictment comes days after Sebastian Zapeta’s arrest and subsequent police questioning, in which authorities say he claimed not to know what had happened but identified himself in photos and surveillance video showing the fire being lit.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Former NCAA quarterback Tommy Lazzaro dies in 'tragic' accident at US Air Force base
item.description: Tommy Lazzaro, an Army sergeant and former quarterback for the Central Michigan Chippewas, has died following an accident at Eglin Air Force Base in Florida. He was 27.
item.dc:creator: Matt Jaworowski, KRON4

item.title: Climate-friendly electricity sees big battery projects soar again for 2024
item.description: 2024 was another banner year for a source of electricity that is better for people's lungs, better for climate change and may be reaching your home when you turn on the lights or turn up the thermostat — large banks of batteries.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN and MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: SEE IT: ‘Peanut-shaped’ roundabout ready to open in Ohio
item.description: In a post to the city's Facebook page, officials offered "a few pointers to help drivers navigate safely.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Langenfeld, KRON4

item.title: China sanctions 7 companies over US military assistance to Taiwan
item.description: The sanctions also come in response to the recent approval of the U.S. government's annual defense spending bill, which a Chinese Foreign Ministry statement said “includes multiple negative sections on China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Jackpot for Mega Millions over $1 billion after no numbers match on Christmas Eve
item.description: The estimated jackpot for Friday is $1.15 billion, potentially the fifth-largest jackpot in the game's history.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Powerful thunderstorms rumble across Texas, delaying holiday travel
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — Some flights were delayed or canceled in Texas on Thursday after a line of thunderstorms started moving across parts of the state in a system the National Weather Service predicted could bring high winds, hail and possible tornadoes.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Cat food distributed to 12 states recalled after positive bird flu test; 1 cat dead
item.description: The product was sold through distributors in Arizona, California, Colorado, Florida, Georgia, Illinois, Maryland, Michigan, Minnesota, Pennsylvania, Rhode Island and Wisconsin.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump announces ambassador to Panama amid fight over canal
item.description: President-elect Trump on Wednesday announced Miami-Dade County Commissioner Kevin Marino Cabrera as his pick for U.S. ambassador to Panama. The announcement comes as Trump is sparking a feud with the U.S. ally over control of the Panama Canal.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashes in Kazakhstan leaving 32 survivors
item.description: An Azerbaijani airliner with 67 people onboard crashed Wednesday near the Kazakhstani city of Aktau, leaving at least 32 survivors, according to officials. More than 30 people may be dead.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
